description: set-parameter law (D13 table): the value MUST satisfy the declared type, cardinality, choices, and bounds; out-of-bounds only via Deviation. Tightening direction (C.9): a move against the declared direction requires a Deviation. calendar-period without calendar-ref fails closed (D9). Each vector: parameter declaration + proposed value -> verdict (valid | invalid | deviation-required).

vectors.name: choice-out-of-set-the-md5-evasion
vectors.note: the evasion backdoor P6-F2 closed: without bounds enforcement the typed algebra is theater

vectors.name: integer-wrong-type
vectors.note: type mismatch is a hard error, not a deviation case

vectors.name: tightening-lower-respected
vectors.note: CR26 Class A: one business day -> six hours passes silently

vectors.name: tightening-lower-violated
vectors.note: easing a deadline rides the audited channel

vectors.name: unit-class-crossing-rejected
vectors.note: elapsed vs calendar is a semantic boundary, not a spelling (C.9; backlog #3 closed no-change at 0 measured crossings)

vectors.name: calendar-period-needs-calendar
vectors.note: declaration itself is malformed: calendar-ref is required - representable without context, computable only with it

vectors.name: decimal-as-float-rejected
vectors.note: IEEE-754 never enters the kernel (D3.4 - the JCS float hazard)

vectors.name: default-is-advisory-never-binds
vectors.note: an unset parameter stays unset - resolution never substitutes the default silently (D9 rev); binding is set-parameter-only, and null is not a binding
